Title: Purification and biochemical characterization of GTs

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to engineer glycosylation pattern and stabilization of betalain/flavonoid pigments; useful for redirecting vacuolar sink formation and pigment modification, but broad acceptor specificity means metabolite off-targets should be evaluated.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to betanidin mainly at the 5-hydroxyl position, and can also glucosylate quercetin at 4'-OH and 7-OH.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of betanidin 5-O-glucoside/betacyanin glycosides and can glycosylate flavonols.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Enzyme purification; heterologous expression in E. coli; substrate specificity and kinetic assays; molecular modeling.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Broad acceptor specificity; quercetin accepted with comparable kinetics; regioselective rather than absolutely substrate-specific.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible unintended glycosylation of related flavonoids due to broad acceptor range.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase; PSPG-box-containing plant secondary product GT
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Purified and cloned as a PSPG-box glycosyltransferase involved in betacyanin formation. 2. Glucosylates betanidin at 5-OH and also accepts flavonol quercetin. 3. Demonstrates broad acceptor specificity but strong regioselectivity.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation and potential stress-related protective roles.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for tailoring pigment glycosylation profile in metabolic engineering, but broad substrate preference necessitates metabolite profiling.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to betanidin at the 6-hydroxyl position; also glucosylates quercetin specifically at 3-OH and cyanidin efficiently.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of betanidin 6-O-glucoside-type products and accepts cyanidin/flavonol substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Enzyme purification; substrate specificity assays; molecular modeling.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Best substrate reported was cyanidin rather than quercetin; highlights mismatch between presumed natural substrate and in vitro best substrate.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad activity could perturb other flavonoid pools.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase; PSPG-box-containing plant secondary product GT
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Purified enzyme distinct from betanidin 5 GT. 2. Shows strict regioselectivity but broad acceptor preference, including cyanidin and quercetin. 3. Supports concept that plant GTs are regioselective rather than absolutely substrate-specific.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering cyanogenic glucoside stabilization and defense chemistry, but deployment requires caution because cyanogenic metabolites are toxic and affect food/feed safety.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to p-hydroxymandelonitrile (and related cyanohydrins), forming the cyanogenic glucoside dhurrin; also accepts mandelonitrile, benzyl alcohol and geraniol in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes dhurrin formation and stabilization of cyanogenic intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Enzyme purification; cloning; heterologous expression in E. coli; substrate specificity/kinetic assays.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unexpectedly glucosylates geraniol in vitro; illustrates broad acceptor scope despite physiological role in dhurrin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation may alter cyanide-related defense/toxicity balance and have food safety implications.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase; PSPG-box-containing plant secondary product GT
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant for stable cyanogenic glucoside formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Purified and molecularly characterized enzyme catalyzes the final glucosylation step in dhurrin biosynthesis. 2. Accepts several non-native substrates in vitro, but physiological role is cyanogenic glucoside formation. 3. Demonstrates broad acceptor specificity coupled with donor specificity for UDP-glucose.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chemical defense and storage/stabilization of cyanogenic potential.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for citrus quality improvement by reducing bitterness through controlled expression in fruit; useful for breeding less bitter pummelo/Citrus varieties.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to limonoids at the 17-OH position, producing limonoid glucosides.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes glucosylation of bitter limonoids and contributes to debittering.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Enzyme purification; cloning; heterologous expression in E. coli; substrate assays.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Clustered phylogenetically with carboxyl-group glycosylating enzymes despite limonoid substrate assignment.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May affect defense-related limonoid aglycone pools while improving taste.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase; PSPG-box-containing plant secondary product GT
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Potential terminal determinant for bitterness-related product conversion
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Citrus limonoid GT glucosylates bitter limonoids to form glucosides. 2. The gene is highlighted as a biotechnology target for fruit quality improvement. 3. Sequence groups with other acid/ester-forming GTs, suggesting shared recognition features.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Reduces bitterness; affects fruit quality and palatability.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to modify pollen flavonol glycosides and possibly reproductive traits or pigment-associated antioxidant profiles; tissue specificity may be valuable for targeted engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galactose from UDP-galactose to flavonols at the 3-hydroxyl position.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein purification; cloning; heterologous expression in E. coli; substrate assays.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Membrane-associated exception among mostly soluble plant secondary product GTs.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered flavonol glycosylation could affect pollen performance.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Petunia pollen GalT is a pollen-specific flavonol glycosyltransferase. 2. It transfers galactose to flavonols at 3-OH. 3. It represents a membrane-associated exception within largely soluble GTs of secondary metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Likely contributes to pollen function and flavonoid stabilization.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential tool to modulate salicylic acid homeostasis and defense-related metabolite conjugation, but could alter immunity signaling if overexpressed.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to salicylic acid and benzoic/p-hydroxybenzoic acid, glycosylating hydroxyl or carboxyl groups to form conjugates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of salicylic acid and benzoic acid glucose conjugates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Purification; cloning; heterologous expression in E. coli; enzyme assays.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms conjugated storage/inactive derivatives of signaling metabolites.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reported to show higher affinity for benzoic or p-hydroxybenzoic acid acyl groups than for the in vivo substrate salicylic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May dampen defense signaling by conjugating salicylic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Salicylic acid glucoside and related benzoate conjugates
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Salicylic acid; benzoic acid; p-hydroxybenzoic acid; UDP-glucose
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Condition-dependent controller of salicylate conjugation, not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tobacco salicylic acid GT is pathogen inducible and glucosylates salicylic acid-related compounds. 2. In vitro affinity is broader and may favor benzoates over the presumed natural substrate. 3. The enzyme exemplifies broad acceptor specificity among PSPG-box GTs.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for manipulating sterol conjugation and membrane-associated metabolite traits, though physiological effects on membrane composition and growth must be considered.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to sterols at the 3-hydroxyl position, producing steryl glucosides.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Purification; biochemical characterization; later cloning and yeast expression verification.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Membrane-bound enzyme with unusual solubility and activity in diethyl ether.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changing sterol glycosylation may impact membrane properties and development.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Oat sterol GT was purified owing to unique solubility/activity properties. 2. It glucosylates sterols at the 3-OH position. 3. Biochemical assignment was later confirmed by cloning and heterologous expression.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for engineering flower/fruit pigmentation and flavonoid stabilization.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to flavonols and anthocyanidins at the 3-hydroxyl position.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cDNA cloning by GT-probe screening; heterologous expression in Lisianthus.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included in well-characterized cluster I of flavonoid 3-GTs.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter multiple flavonoid glycosides due to shared acceptor range.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A recombinant Antirrhinum GT glucosylates flavonols and anthocyanidins at 3-OH. 2. It exemplifies cDNA-first characterization of plant secondary product GTs. 3. It belongs to the flavonoid 3-GT sequence cluster.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering anthocyanin accumulation and flower color.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to anthocyanidins at 3-OH.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cDNA cloning and expression in E. coli.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Gentiana 3-GT glucosylates anthocyanidins at 3-OH. 2. It was cloned and expressed in E. coli. 3. It belongs to the anthocyanidin/flavonol 3-GT cluster.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highlighted as a biotechnology target to modulate berry coloration and potentially distinguish red from white grape varieties once transformation is feasible.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to anthocyanidins and flavonols at 3-OH.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with anthocyanin accumulation and red berry pigmentation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cDNA cloning; expression in E. coli; transcript association with anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific GT transcript may distinguish red and white grape varieties.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Pigment pathway engineering may alter broader flavonoid composition.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Potential terminal determinant for stable anthocyanin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Grapevine 3GT glucosylates anthocyanidins/flavonols at 3-OH. 2. Its expression correlates with anthocyanin accumulation. 3. It is proposed as a target for modifying grape color and quality.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for tuning anthocyanin glycosylation patterns and pigment stability.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to anthocyanidins at 3-OH.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cDNA cloning; heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Comparison with Perilla 5-GT helps infer amino acids controlling regiospecificity.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Perilla 3GT is an anthocyanidin 3-O-glucosyltransferase. 2. It was functionally expressed in yeast. 3. It serves as a comparator for understanding regiospecificity relative to 5-GTs.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to tailor anthocyanin 5-O-glycosylation and pigment stability in engineered plants.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to anthocyanins at 5-OH.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cDNA cloning; heterologous expression in S. cerevisiae.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Perilla 5GTs catalyze anthocyanin 5-O-glucosylation. 2. They form a distinct sequence cluster from 3-GTs. 3. Comparison with 3-GTs helps define regiospecificity determinants.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for flower color engineering and for comparative studies of regiospecificity determinants.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to anthocyanidins at 3-OH.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Position in cluster I matched predicted location.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Petunia 3-GT catalyzes anthocyanidin 3-O-glucosylation. 2. It fits cluster I of flavonoid 3-GTs. 3. It is useful for comparing determinants of acceptor/regiospecificity with petunia 5-GT.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering anthocyanin modification and flower color traits.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to anthocyanins at 5-OH.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Related to cluster II, consistent with regiospecificity similarity to Perilla 5-GTs.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Petunia 5-GT catalyzes anthocyanin 5-O-glucosylation. 2. Its phylogenetic position agrees with its regiospecificity. 3. It provides a close comparison with petunia 3-GT for structure-function studies.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for producing medicinal flavonoid glycosides and altering antioxidant metabolite profiles.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to flavonols/flavones at 7-OH; scutellarein 7-O-glucosylation highlighted.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli; functional analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Clusters with betanidin 5-GT and stress-inducible Solanaceae homologs, suggesting sequence conservation linked more to regioselectivity than substrate class.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Scutellaria 7-GT catalyzes 7-O-glucosylation of flavonoids. 2. It groups with other GTs sharing related regioselectivity despite different preferred substrates. 3. This supports the idea that sequence clusters reflect regioselectivity.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for detoxification engineering and manipulation of glycoalkaloid storage/toxicity; should be used cautiously because glycoalkaloids affect pest resistance and food safety.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to steroidal glycoalkaloids/solanidine at the 3-OH position.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of steroidal glycoalkaloid glucosides and reduces solanidine toxicity in recombinant yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae; detoxification phenotype; sequence analysis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/detoxification form of target alkaloid.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: N-terminus proposed to contribute steroid-binding specificity.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation can alter toxicity, pest resistance, and food safety traits.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase; N-terminus proposed steroid-binding domain
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant for detoxified glycoside formation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Potato solanidine GT glucosylates steroidal glycoalkaloids. 2. Expression in yeast detoxifies solanidine, demonstrating functional activity. 3. The N-terminus is implicated in acceptor specificity for steroid substrates.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Classic gene for modifying flavonoid glycosylation and pigmentation traits; useful as a reference for GT family studies.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Putatively transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to flavonols at the 3-OH position.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning by transposon tagging; phenotypic characterization; sequence homology.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: First cDNA sequence of a plant secondary-metabolism GT to become available; function described as putative in this review.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Pigment/flavonoid changes may affect stress responses and coloration.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Bronze-1 was the first cloned plant secondary-metabolism GT cDNA. 2. It encodes a putative flavonol 3-O-glucosyltransferase. 3. It served as a template for cloning related GTs by homology.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for growth modulation by altering hormone glycosylation; requires caution because it may strongly affect development.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose to indole-3-acetic acid, forming IAA glucose conjugates.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes IAA glycosylation and hormone homeostasis modification.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms conjugated storage/inactive derivative of hormone metabolite.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Grouped with salicylic acid GT and limonoid GT; may preferentially glycosylate carboxyl groups.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Developmental defects may arise from altered auxin homeostasis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Maize IAA GT is phylogenetically close to salicylic acid and limonoid GTs. 2. This suggests shared recognition properties for acid substrates. 3. It illustrates breadth of metabolite classes handled by PSPG-box GTs.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Sequence resource for predicting GT function and potential manipulation of specialized metabolite glycosides, but function remained unvalidated in this review.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Putative UDP-sugar-dependent glycosyl transfer to secondary metabolites; exact reaction not functionally established here.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Not functionally validated in the paper; used for consensus sequence/phylogenetic analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Sequence alignment and motif analysis only.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Cassava sequences contributed to proposal of the PSPG consensus sequence.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PSPG-box-containing putative plant secondary product GT
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cassava CGT sequences were used to define the PSPG consensus motif. 2. They are putative GTs without functional characterization in this review. 3. They helped expand the recognized plant GT superfamily.
